headline: RM2.3 billion flood mitigation funds spent but floods worsen annually
context: The federal government spent RM2.3 billion on flood mitigation between 2022-2025, yet flood damage increased 40% over the same period according to NADMA data.

cards.big: RM2.3 billion spent on flood mitigation but flood damage got 40% worse
cards.sub: Four years of spending with measurably negative outcomes across Peninsular Malaysia

cards.big: DID projects undergo no mandatory post-completion impact assessment
cards.sub: No requirement to verify if completed mitigation infrastructure actually reduces flooding

cards.big: Flood damage costs rose from RM6.1 billion in 2022 to RM8.5 billion in 2025
cards.sub: Every ringgit spent on mitigation corresponded to RM1.04 in increased damage

cards.big: 3 separate agencies manage flood response with no unified command structure
cards.sub: DID, JPS, and NADMA have overlapping mandates and competing budget requests

cards.big: Spending money on floods isn't the same as solving floods — accountability requires outcomes
Should flood mitigation funding be tied to measurable reduction in damage outcomes?
